title: SpecSchema
description: Canonical spec contract for agent orchestration. Every downstream component validates against this.

goal.description: One sentence. Subject + verb + object. No conjunctions. Rejected by validator if over 20 words.

success_criteria.description: Measurable outcomes. Each item must contain a number, threshold, or observable state. Ban list (not enforced here): good, appropriate, reasonable, correct, proper.

constraints.description: Hard limits on behaviour. Only include constraints you can write a test case for.

preconditions.description: What must be true before execution begins. Checked at runtime — pipeline halts with a clear message if any precondition is unmet.

postconditions.description: What must be true after execution, independent of path taken. System states, not business outcomes.

test_cases.description: Input/output pairs. Minimum: 1 passing case + 1 failing case. Failing case is mandatory — proves the spec is testable.

input.description: The input fed to the component under test.

expected_output.description: The exact output or observable state expected.

type.description: passing = this input should succeed. failing = this input must be rejected.

non_goals.description: Explicit list of what this spec does NOT cover. Every boundary must be stated positively — AI cannot infer from omission.

protected.description: Existing things that must not be touched. DB schema, API signatures, auth flows.

signal_budget.description: Maximum number of words the entire spec may contain. Unenforced in this version — field exists so the interviewer can populate it. Enforcement added in a later step.
